77	// reVerdictLine matches "### Verdict: APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK"
78	// anywhere in the output, case-insensitive, allowing 1–4 leading hashes.
79	var reVerdictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`)
80	var reReviewSectionHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+(AC Grades|Summary|Findings)\s*:?\s*$`)
81	var reACReference = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\bAC#\s*([0-9]+)\b`)
82	
83	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable is returned by ParseReviewVerdictStrict when
84	// the reviewer output does not contain a recognizable `### Verdict: ...`
85	// line. Callers should surface this as a review-error (retryable) rather
86	// than silently treating it as BLOCK — the pre-ddx-f7ae036f behavior was to
87	// default-to-BLOCK, which produced spurious APPROVE→BLOCK mis-records
88	// whenever the reviewer's output shape drifted (codex stream frames,
89	// rationale-only responses, stall-truncated output).
90	var ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable = fmt.Errorf("reviewer output: no recognizable verdict line")
91	
92	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict is the structured-error variant of
93	// ParseReviewVerdict: on unparseable input it returns
94	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable instead of silently returning VerdictBlock.
95	// The execute-loop uses this path so a parse failure reopens the bead for
96	// retry rather than recording a false BLOCK.
97	func ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output string) (ReviewVerdict, error) {
98		m := reVerdictLine.FindStringSubmatch(output)
99		if m == nil {
100			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
101		}
102		switch strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(m[1])) {
103		case "APPROVE":
104			return VerdictApprove, nil
105		case "REQUEST_CHANGES":
106			return VerdictRequestChanges, nil
107		case "BLOCK":
108			return VerdictBlock, nil
109		default:
110			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
111		}
112	}
113	
114	// ParseReviewVerdict extracts the verdict from a review agent's output.
115	// The expected format includes a line: ### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
116	//
117	// Deprecated: returns VerdictBlock on unparseable input, which masks
118	// legitimate verdict-parse failures as BLOCKs and caused the wave-2
119	// review-malfunction incident (ddx-f7ae036f). New callers should use
120	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict and treat ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a
121	// retryable review-error.
122	func ParseReviewVerdict(output string) ReviewVerdict {
123		v, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
124		if err != nil {
125			return VerdictBlock
126		}
127		return v
128	}

130	func ParseReviewResult(output string) (ReviewVerdict, []ReviewAC, string) {
131		verdict := ParseReviewVerdict(output)
132		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
133		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
134		if len(perAC) == 0 {
135			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict, rationale)
136		}
137		return verdict, perAC, rationale
138	}

140	func parseReviewACTable(output string) []ReviewAC {
141		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
142		var rows []ReviewAC
143		inTable := false
144		for _, raw := range lines {
145			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
146			if line == "" {
147				if inTable {
148					break
149				}
150				continue
151			}
152			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
153				inTable = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(line), "ac grades")
154				continue
155			}
156			if !inTable || !strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
157				continue
158			}
159			cols := splitMarkdownTableRow(line)
160			if len(cols) < 4 {
161				continue
162			}
163			if cols[0] == "#" || strings.HasPrefix(cols[0], "---") {
164				continue
165			}
166			n, err := strconv.Atoi(cols[0])
167			if err != nil {
168				continue
169			}
170			rows = append(rows, ReviewAC{
171				Number:   n,
172				Item:     cols[1],
173				Grade:    cols[2],
174				Evidence: cols[3],
175			})
176		}
177		return rows
178	}
179	
180	func splitMarkdownTableRow(line string) []string {
181		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
182		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
183		for _, part := range parts {
184			part = strings.TrimSpace(part)
185			if part == "" {
186				continue
187			}
188			out = append(out, part)
189		}
190		return out
191	}

193	func extractReviewRationale(output string, perAC []ReviewAC) string {
194		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
195		var summaryLines []string
196		var findingLines []string
197		var section string
198		for _, raw := range lines {
199			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
200			if line == "" {
201				continue
202			}
203			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "## Review:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "### Verdict:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "#### Verdict:") {
204				continue
205			}
206			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
207				heading := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimLeft(line, "# "), ":")))
208				switch heading {
209				case "summary":
210					section = "summary"
211				case "findings":
212					section = "findings"
213				case "ac grades":
214					section = "ac"
215				default:
216					section = ""
217				}
218				continue
219			}
220			if section == "ac" && strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
221				continue
222			}
223			switch section {
224			case "findings":
225				line = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "-"))
226				if line != "" {
227					findingLines = append(findingLines, line)
228				}
229			case "summary":
230				summaryLines = append(summaryLines, line)
231			}
232		}
233		if len(findingLines) > 0 {
234			return strings.Join(findingLines, "\n")
235		}
236		if len(summaryLines) > 0 {
237			return strings.Join(summaryLines, "\n")
238		}
239		var unmet []string
240		for _, ac := range perAC {
241			if strings.EqualFold(ac.Grade, string(VerdictApprove)) {
242				continue
243			}
244			label := fmt.Sprintf("AC#%d", ac.Number)
245			if ac.Item != "" {
246				label += " " + ac.Item
247			}
248			if ac.Evidence != "" {
249				unmet = append(unmet, label+": "+ac.Evidence)
250			} else {
251				unmet = append(unmet, label)
252			}
253		}
254		return strings.Join(unmet, "\n")
255	}

257	func synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict ReviewVerdict, rationale string) []ReviewAC {
258		if rationale == "" {
259			return nil
260		}
261		matches := reACReference.FindAllStringSubmatch(rationale, -1)
262		if len(matches) == 0 {
263			return nil
264		}
265		seen := make(map[int]struct{}, len(matches))
266		out := make([]ReviewAC, 0, len(matches))
267		for _, match := range matches {
268			if len(match) < 2 {
269				continue
270			}
271			n, err := strconv.Atoi(match[1])
272			if err != nil {
273				continue
274			}
275			if _, ok := seen[n]; ok {
276				continue
277			}
278			seen[n] = struct{}{}
279			out = append(out, ReviewAC{
280				Number:   n,
281				Grade:    string(verdict),
282				Evidence: rationale,
283			})
284		}
285		return out
286	}

322	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
323	// The reviewing agent must produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK with
324	// the exact markdown format described below.
325	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
326	
327	## Your task
328	
329	Examine the diff and each acceptance-criteria (AC) item. For each item assign one grade:
330	
331	- **APPROVE** — fully and correctly implemented; cite the specific file path and line that proves it.
332	- **REQUEST_CHANGES** — partially implemented or has fixable minor issues.
333	- **BLOCK** — not implemented, incorrectly implemented, or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
334	
335	Overall verdict rule:
336	- All items APPROVE → **APPROVE**
337	- Any item BLOCK → **BLOCK**
338	- Otherwise → **REQUEST_CHANGES**
339	
340	## Required output format
341	
342	Respond with a structured review using exactly this layout (replace placeholder text):
343	
344	---
345	## Review: <bead-id> iter <N>
346	
347	### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
348	
349	### AC Grades
350	
351	| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
352	|---|------|-------|----------|
353	| 1 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | APPROVE | path/to/file.go:42 — brief note |
354	| 2 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | BLOCK   | — not found in diff |
355	
356	### Summary
357	
358	<1–3 sentences on overall implementation quality and any recurring theme in findings.>
359	
360	### Findings
361	
362	<Bullet list of REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK findings. Each finding must name the specific file, function, or test that is missing or wrong — specific enough for the next agent to act on without re-reading the entire diff. Omit this section entirely if verdict is APPROVE.>`
363	
364	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
365	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
366	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
367	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
368		Caps evidence.Caps
369	}
370	
371	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
372	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
373	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
374	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
375	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
376		Prompt   string
377		Overflow bool
378		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
379	}
380	
381	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
382	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
383	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
384	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
385		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
386	}
387	
388	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
389	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
390	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
391	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
392	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
393	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
394	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
395	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
396		caps := opts.Caps
397		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
398			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
399		}
400	
401		var sb strings.Builder
402		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
403	
404		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
405	
406		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
407		beadStart := sb.Len()
408		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
409		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
410	
411		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
412			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
413		} else {
414			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
415		}
416	
417		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
418			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
419		} else {
420			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
421		}
422	
423		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
424			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
425		}
426	
427		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
428			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
429		}
430	
431		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
432		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
433			Name:          "bead",
434			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
435			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
436		})
437	
438		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
439		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
440		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
441			invStart := sb.Len()
442			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
443			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
444			for _, f := range allFiles {
445				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
446				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
447			}
448			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
449			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
450				Name:          "changed-files",
451				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
452				SelectedItems: paths,
453			})
454		}
455	
456		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
457		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
458		if len(refs) == 0 {
459			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
460		} else {
461			for _, ref := range refs {
462				if ref.Title != "" {
463					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
464				} else {
465					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
466				}
467				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
468				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
469				if readErr != nil {
470					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
471					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
472						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
473						TruncationReason: "read_error",
474						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
475					})
476				} else {
477					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
478					if truncated {
479						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
480					}
481					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", txt)
482					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
483						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
484						BytesIncluded: len(content),
485						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
486					}
487					if truncated {
488						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
489						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
490					}
491					sections = append(sections, sec)
492				}
493				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
494			}
495		}
496		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
497	
498		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
499		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
500		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
501		diffSection.Name = "diff"
502		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
503		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n"))
504	
505		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
506		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
507		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
508		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
509	
510		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
511	
512		out := sb.String()
513		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
514			Prompt:   out,
515			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
516			Sections: sections,
517		}
518	}
519	
520	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
521	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
522	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
523	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
524	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
525	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
526	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
527		if len(files) == 0 {
528			return originalDiff
529		}
530		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
531			if f.Path == "" {
532				return 3
533			}
534			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
535				return 0
536			}
537			for _, r := range refs {
538				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
539					return 1
540				}
541			}
542			return 2
543		}
544		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
545		copy(ordered, files)
546		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
547	
548		var b2 strings.Builder
549		for _, f := range ordered {
550			body := f.Body
551			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
552				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
553			}
554			b2.WriteString(body)
555		}
556		return b2.String()
557	}

559	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
560	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
561	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
562	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
563	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
564		var b strings.Builder
565		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
566		b.WriteString(f.Path)
567		b.WriteString(" b/")
568		b.WriteString(f.Path)
569		b.WriteString("\n")
570		if f.Stat != "" {
571			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
572			b.WriteString("\n")
573		}
574		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
575			b.WriteString(h)
576			b.WriteString("\n")
577		}
578		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
579		return b.String()
580	}

69	// TestParseReviewVerdictStrict covers ddx-f7ae036f AC #3 and #4: the strict
70	// variant returns a typed error on unparseable input instead of silently
71	// collapsing to BLOCK. Callers that propagate the error get review-error
72	// (retryable) event-loop handling; callers that swallow it get the same
73	// legacy BLOCK default. The difference is the caller's choice, not a silent
74	// mis-record.
75	func TestParseReviewVerdictStrict(t *testing.T) {
76		tests := []struct {
77			name    string
78			input   string
79			want    ReviewVerdict
80			wantErr bool
81		}{
82			{
83				name:    "clean APPROVE",
84				input:   "some preamble\n### Verdict: APPROVE\n\ntrailing",
85				want:    VerdictApprove,
86				wantErr: false,
87			},
88			{
89				name:    "clean BLOCK",
90				input:   "### Verdict: BLOCK\n",
91				want:    VerdictBlock,
92				wantErr: false,
93			},
94			{
95				name:    "clean REQUEST_CHANGES",
96				input:   "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n",
97				want:    VerdictRequestChanges,
98				wantErr: false,
99			},
100			{
101				name:    "empty output",
102				input:   "",
103				wantErr: true,
104			},
105			{
106				name:    "unparseable — no verdict line",
107				input:   "Reviewer crashed mid-stream.\nNo structured verdict anywhere.",
108				wantErr: true,
109			},
110			{
111				name:    "unparseable — lowercase inline verdict without heading",
112				input:   "I would say verdict: APPROVE but I haven't formatted it",
113				wantErr: true,
114			},
115		}
116		for _, tc := range tests {
117			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
118				got, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(tc.input)
119				if tc.wantErr {
120					assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable,
121						"unparseable input must surface a typed error — the silent default-to-BLOCK behavior is what caused the f7ae036f incident")
122					return
123				}
124				assert.NoError(t, err)
125				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, got)
126			})
127		}
128	}

495	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
496	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
497	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
498	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
499	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
500	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {
501		// Use a single-bead store so we can assert "exactly one attempt".
502		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
503		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
504		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-bound-1", Title: "Impossible AC"}
505		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
506	
507		executorCalls := 0
508		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
509			Store: store,
510			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
511				executorCalls++
512				return ExecuteBeadReport{
513					BeadID:    beadID,
514					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
515					SessionID: "sess-bound",
516					ResultRev: "11111111",
517				}, nil
518			}),
519			Reviewer: makeReviewer(VerdictRequestChanges, "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n\nStill failing."),
520		}
521	
522		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
523			Assignee: "worker",
524			// no Once flag: drain the queue fully within this run
525		})
526		require.NoError(t, err)
527	
528		// The executor should have been called exactly once; the attempted map
529		// prevents revisiting even after the bead is reopened by the reviewer.
530		assert.Equal(t, 1, executorCalls, "bead should be attempted exactly once within a single worker.Run call")
531		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
532		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures, "reopened bead counts as a failure in this run")
533	
534		// Bead is open again — available for the next worker.Run call.
535		got, err := store.Get(only.ID)
536		require.NoError(t, err)
537		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
538	}

565	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
566	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
567	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
568	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
569	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
570	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
571	//
572	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
573	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
574	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
575	// and stays bounded.
576	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
577		root := t.TempDir()

944	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
945	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
946	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
947	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
948	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
949	//     embed the class in their message).
950	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
951	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
952	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
953	//     matches.
954	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
955		if reviewRes != nil {
956			switch reviewRes.Error {
957			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
958				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
959				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
960				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
961				return reviewRes.Error
962			}
963		}
964		msg := ""
965		if reviewErr != nil {
966			msg = reviewErr.Error()
967		}
968		switch {
969		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
970			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
971		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
972			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
973		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
974			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
975		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
976			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
977		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
978			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
979		}
980		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
981	}

16	// TestReviewErrorTaxonomy covers FEAT-022 §12 / Stage G AC #1: each of the
17	// four review-error classes is emitted distinctly when its root cause fires,
18	// and the appended review-error event body carries the literal class
19	// identifier so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
20	func TestReviewErrorTaxonomy(t *testing.T) {
21		tests := []struct {
22			name      string
23			wantClass string
24			reviewer  BeadReviewerFunc
25		}{
26			{
27				name:      "context_overflow",
28				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
29				// Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit returns ReviewResult.Error
30				// = OutcomeReviewContextOverflow alongside a wrapped error. The
31				// loop classifier reads the structured Error first.
32				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
33					return &ReviewResult{
34							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
35							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
36							ResultRev: resultRev,
37						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt 200000 bytes exceeds cap 100000)",
38							evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow)
39				}),
40			},
41			{
42				name:      "provider_empty",
43				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
44				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
45					return &ReviewResult{
46							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
47							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
48							ResultRev: resultRev,
49						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
50							ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
51				}),
52			},
53			{
54				name:      "unparseable",
55				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
56				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
57					return &ReviewResult{
58							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
59							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
60							ResultRev: resultRev,
61							RawOutput: "Reviewer text without a structured verdict line.",
62						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
63							ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
64				}),
65			},
66			{
67				name:      "transport",
68				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
69				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
70					return &ReviewResult{
71							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
72							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
73							ResultRev: resultRev,
74						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
75							errors.New("dial tcp: connection refused"))
76				}),
77			},
78		}
79	
80		for _, tc := range tests {
81			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
82				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
83				worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
84					Store: store,
85					Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
86						return ExecuteBeadReport{
87							BeadID:    beadID,
88							Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
89							SessionID: "sess-tax-" + tc.name,
90							ResultRev: "rev-" + tc.name,
91						}, nil
92					}),
93					Reviewer: tc.reviewer,
94				}
95	
96				_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
97				require.NoError(t, err)
98	
99				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
100				require.NoError(t, err)
101				var got *bead.BeadEvent
102				for i := range events {
103					if events[i].Kind == "review-error" {
104						got = &events[i]
105						break
106					}
107				}
108				require.NotNil(t, got, "expected a review-error event for class %s", tc.wantClass)
109				// AC #1: event body must contain the literal class identifier.
110				assert.Contains(t, got.Body, tc.wantClass,
111					"review-error event body must carry the literal class string for operator triage")
112				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantClass, got.Summary,
113					"review-error event summary should be the class identifier")
114				// And the result_rev association must be recorded so the retry
115				// counter can be scoped to the originating commit.
116				assert.True(t, strings.Contains(got.Body, "result_rev=rev-"+tc.name),
117					"event body must include result_rev= for retry-counter scoping")
118			})
119		}
120	}

30	// seedReviewErrorEvents pre-populates a bead with N existing review-error
31	// events scoped to the given result_rev, simulating prior failed reviewer
32	// attempts. Used to drive the retry counter to its threshold without running
33	// the loop N-1 extra times.
34	func seedReviewErrorEvents(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, resultRev, class string, n int) {
35		t.Helper()
36		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
37			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
38				Kind:      "review-error",
39				Summary:   class,
40				Body:      reviewErrorEventBody(class, i+1, resultRev, "prior failure"),
41				Actor:     "worker",
42				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
43				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
44			}))
45		}
46	}

48	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired covers FEAT-022 §14
49	// case (a): after N (default 3) review-error events on the same result_rev,
50	// the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and parks the
51	// bead so a subsequent execute-loop iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
52	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired(t *testing.T) {
53		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
54		const resultRev = "deadbeef"
55	
56		// Seed N-1 prior failures (default N=3 → 2 prior events). The loop's
57		// reviewer attempt becomes the 3rd, which must trip the terminal event.
58		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, resultRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty, 2)
59	
60		var execCount atomic.Int32
61		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
62			Store: store,
63			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
64				if beadID == first.ID {
65					execCount.Add(1)
66				}
67				return ExecuteBeadReport{
68					BeadID:    beadID,
69					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
70					SessionID: "sess-r1",
71					ResultRev: resultRev,
72				}, nil
73			}),
74			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty),
75		}
76	
77		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
78		require.NoError(t, err)
79		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(), "primary should run exactly once on this iteration")
80	
81		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
82		require.NoError(t, err)
83	
84		var manual *bead.BeadEvent
85		for i := range events {
86			if events[i].Kind == "review-manual-required" {
87				manual = &events[i]
88				break
89			}
90		}
91		require.NotNil(t, manual, "Nth review failure must emit a terminal review-manual-required event")
92		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
93			"manual-required body must include failure_class for triage")
94		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "attempt_count=3",
95			"manual-required body must include attempt_count for triage")
96		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
97			"manual-required body must include the blocked result_rev for triage")
98	
99		// §13 invariant: bead must NOT be closed by reviewer-failure escalation.
100		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
101		require.NoError(t, err)
102		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
103			"reviewer failure (even at terminal escalation) must never close the bead")
104	
105		// Subsequent execute-loop iteration must NOT re-execute primary. The
106		// bead is parked via SetExecutionCooldown so ReadyExecution skips it.
107		_, err = worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
108		require.NoError(t, err)
109		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(),
110			"after review-manual-required the executor must not be invoked again")
111	}

data.text:
Commit landed. All AC requirements satisfied:

- **AC1**: `cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go` defines `ReviewVerdict` struct with `Verdict` (enum-validated), `Findings []Finding`, `Summary`, `SchemaVersion` fields.
- **AC2**: `ParseReviewVerdict` accepts raw/fenced/mixed JSON; rejects unknown verdicts, missing field, truncated JSON.
- **AC3**: `TestParseReviewVerdict` passes with one subtest per case.
- **AC4**: `TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract` passes — markdown rejected with `unparseable` class.
- **AC5**: `TestReviewerJSONHappyPath` passes — JSON contract decodes to APPROVE.
- **AC6**: `TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON` passes — template requests JSON with schema example, no legacy AC-grades table.
- **AC7**: `TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression` passes — options-header echoes are rejected, never silently parsed as BLOCK.
